claims.summary: A 31-second stall can coexist with a seven-second generator if rescue or channel queueing delays delivery.
claims.disposition: Implemented separate rescue-admission and actual channel-enqueue evidence, including queue wait and inter-enqueue gaps; downstream processing remains explicitly unproven.

claims.summary: Rescue rejection should be correlated with heartbeat gaps.
claims.disposition: Verified block reasons, skip rate, pressure context, and generated-without-delivered signal evidence are durably recorded.

claims.summary: The loop should expose phase/timestamp progress even when perceived continuity stalls.
claims.disposition: Verified each attempt records phase step and wall time; the new enqueue receipt adds completion time and gap evidence without adding noisy raw logs.

claims.summary: Heartbeat intensity should increase from 0.30 to 0.45 to overcome entropy.
claims.disposition: Preserved as Tier 5 because intensity changes live sensory gain and requires explicit operator approval and a bounded canary.
